learning_outcomes.description: Understand Professional Roles and Reflective Practice. Describe the role of occupational therapists in contemporary practice across various contexts and engage in self-reflection and reflective practice to identify personal values, beliefs, and interpersonal characteristics that influence professional practice.

Develop Communication and Therapeutic Skills.Demonstrate foundational therapeutic communication skills necessary for professional communication, including interviewing, therapeutic use of self, and the ability to adapt and critique communication approaches to support client needs/client engagement and occupational performance.
Demonstrate knowledge of methods in assessing components of neurological function (such as sensory evaluation, tone, and coordination).
Apply theoretical knowledge of human anatomy to identify and palpate anatomical structures on the living body and perform range of motion (ROM) measurement and manual muscle testing (MMT).
Demonstrate effective communication skills when handling and assessing individuals.
Demonstrate professional skills including the ability to evaluate learning and performance of self and others and to work collaboratively within a team.

assessments.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
Students will undertake an interview with a peer during Week 7. The interviews will be audio-recorded for moderating purposes, and assessed in person by a marker on the day. The peer whom you will interview will be decided by assessors on the day of the interview, just prior to the interview.


Refer to the task description and marking rubric in the Assessment Folder on Learn.UQ for further details.


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempt to use AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.


Recording of assessment

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. Assessment Procedure (95)).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
28/10/2026 1:00 pm

The OCTY7811 ROM/MMT practical vivas will occur from 1pm to 5pm. Students may be scheduled at any time between 1pm and 5pm. The course coordinator will provide information about the viva schedule closer to the time, and this information will be uploaded to Learn.UQ
assessments.task_description:
This practical viva exam assesses a core skill in occupational therapy, and students must achieve a passing grade of 5 or above (> 65%) to pass the assessment item.

Students will be asked to perform one range-of-motion and one manual muscle test on a peer.

 

Students will receive an automatic fail on that test if they assess the incorrect limb (left vs. right) or the incorrect movement, as these are considered critical clinical errors.


In the event of a disruption that prevents the scheduled assessment from taking place as planned, the assessment will be changed to a Zoom-invigilated viva exam. The timing of the assessment may also be impacted.


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative artificial intelligence (AI) or machine translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempt to use AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.


Recording of assessment

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. Assessment Procedure (95)).

assessments.task_description:
In-person, online central exam using Inspera (with Safe Exam Browser) to be held during the end-of-semester exam block. This exam will be based on the learning and reflection questions from the PROFESSIONAL COMMUNICATION content. The exam format will include the learning and reflection questions discussed throughout the semester.

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempt to use AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students will need to bring their personal computers to complete the Inspera exam and have the Safe Exam Browser downloaded. For more information, please refer to the link provided: https://elearning.uq.edu.au/student-guides-original/inspera-assessment-original/safe-exam-browser-inspera-original

learning_activities.topic:
Professional communcation

Professional Communication is a 2-hour, in-person, theory-driven tutorial held on Wednesdays from 8am to 10am during weeks 1–13 as part of the OCTY7808 course. Students enrolled in OCTY7811 are required to attend the professional communication stream within OCTY7808.

These tutorials are not recorded and are designed as interactive, experiential learning sessions. They focus on developing the professional communication skills and professionalism required for effective therapeutic communication and success as an occupational therapist. Students are expected to attend all sessions, participate actively, and engage in both individual and peer-based reflection. The tutorials provide opportunities for small-group practice and refinement of specific communication skills.

A detailed schedule of tutorial topics will be available on Learn.UQ.

learning_activities.topic:
Functional Anatomy Practicals

The Functional Anatomy Module consists of 3-hour, in-person practical sessions held on Thursdays from 8 am to 11 am during weeks 5–12.

These practicals are not recorded and are designed as hands-on, experiential learning sessions. They focus on developing students’ understanding of functional anatomy through active exploration, application, and skill development. Students are expected to attend all sessions, participate actively, and engage in practical activities to support their learning.

The sessions provide opportunities for students to apply anatomical knowledge in a functional context, working both individually and collaboratively. Emphasis is placed on linking anatomical structures to movement, function, and occupational performance while learning ROM/MMT assessment skills.

Further details, including a schedule of practical topics, will be available on Learn.UQ.
